name: Login Page
notes: React class component. Shown when AuthContext.isAuthenticated is false.
Auth: public (no authentication required).
Calls AuthContext.login(email, password) on submit.
login() calls POST /api/auth/login then POST /api/auth/refresh; /refresh has a bug that returns session-shaped response causing a crash.
javaEquivalent: Angular 19 page component at /login; Angular AuthService calls HTTP POST to Spring @RestController @PostMapping('/api/auth/login'); Spring Security issues JWT on success; Angular AuthGuard redirects unauthenticated users to /login

name: Login Credentials Form
notes: POST form. onSubmit calls AuthContext.login(email, password).
Fields: email (type=email, required), password (type=password, required).
No client-side validation beyond HTML required attribute.
On error: displays error message from AuthContext.error or caught exception.
Submit button is disabled when AuthContext.loading is true.
javaEquivalent: Angular 19 reactive form (FormGroup with email Validators.email and Validators.required, password Validators.required); (ngSubmit) calls Angular AuthService HTTP POST to Spring @RestController; @Valid on LoginRequest DTO

name: Sign In Button
notes: Submits login credentials to AuthContext.login().
Disabled and shows 'Signing in...' while loading.
On success: AuthContext sets isAuthenticated=true; App renders main workspace.
On failure: error message rendered below the form.
javaEquivalent: Angular (ngSubmit) calls Angular AuthService.login(); HTTP POST to Spring @RestController @PostMapping('/api/auth/login'); on success store JWT in localStorage, Angular Router.navigate(['/'])

name: Main Application Workspace
notes: React class component. Shown when AuthContext.isAuthenticated is true.
Auth: requires authentication; redirects to LoginForm when unauthenticated.
State: activeSection (documents|upload|search|tags), selectedDocument, showPreview.
No URL routing — section changes are pure React state; URL stays at /.
Composed of: Header, Sidebar, SearchBar, DocumentGrid, and conditional PreviewPanel.
DocumentGrid is wired via ref so SearchBar can delegate to DocumentGrid.handleSearch().
javaEquivalent: Angular 19 root shell component at /; Angular AuthGuard protects route; child components data fetched via Angular services (HttpClient) from Spring @RestController endpoints; Angular Router handles section navigation as child routes

name: Application Header
notes: Read-only display panel with three section navigation links.
Receives onNavigate callback from App; calls onNavigate(section) on click.
Sections: 'documents', 'upload', 'search'.
No active-state highlighting on header links (only Sidebar has active highlight).
javaEquivalent: Angular 19 header component with routerLink navigation to Angular routes /documents, /upload, /search; Angular Router handles active link styling

name: Documents Header Link
notes: onClick calls onNavigate('documents').
Sets App.activeSection to 'documents'; highlights Sidebar item.
javaEquivalent: Angular routerLink='/documents'

name: Upload Header Link
notes: onClick calls onNavigate('upload').
Sets App.activeSection to 'upload'; highlights Sidebar item. DocumentGrid upload area is always visible regardless.
javaEquivalent: Angular routerLink='/upload'

name: Search Header Link
notes: onClick calls onNavigate('search').
Sets App.activeSection to 'search'; highlights Sidebar item.
javaEquivalent: Angular routerLink='/search'

name: Navigation Sidebar
notes: Vertical navigation panel with four sections.
Receives activeSection and onNavigate from App.
Active section highlighted with different background color.
Sections: 'documents' (All Documents), 'upload' (Upload), 'search' (Search), 'tags' (Tags).
Tags section has no corresponding view — same DocumentGrid shown for all sections.
javaEquivalent: Angular 19 sidebar component with routerLink and routerLinkActive for /documents, /upload, /search, /tags routes

name: All Documents Sidebar Link
notes: onClick calls onNavigate('documents').
Highlighted when activeSection === 'documents'.
javaEquivalent: Angular routerLink='/documents' with routerLinkActive='active'

name: Upload Sidebar Link
notes: onClick calls onNavigate('upload').
Highlighted when activeSection === 'upload'.
javaEquivalent: Angular routerLink='/upload' with routerLinkActive='active'

name: Search Sidebar Link
notes: onClick calls onNavigate('search').
Highlighted when activeSection === 'search'.
javaEquivalent: Angular routerLink='/search' with routerLinkActive='active'

name: Tags Sidebar Link
notes: onClick calls onNavigate('tags').
Highlighted when activeSection === 'tags'.
No dedicated Tags view exists — same DocumentGrid is displayed for this section.
javaEquivalent: Angular routerLink='/tags' with routerLinkActive='active'; target Angular /tags route should render a dedicated tag management component

name: Global Search Bar
notes: GET-style form rendered in the search container between header and main content.
Fields: query (type=text, placeholder='Search documents...', required non-empty).
onSubmit calls onSearch(query) prop; App.handleSearch() delegates to DocumentGrid.handleSearch() via ref.
DocumentGrid.handleSearch() calls GET /api/search?q={query}.
If query is empty after trim, no action taken.
javaEquivalent: Angular 19 reactive form with search input; (ngSubmit) calls Angular DocumentService.search(query) HTTP GET to Spring @RestController @GetMapping('/api/search') with @RequestParam String q

name: Search Submit Button
notes: Submits search query to DocumentGrid.handleSearch().
Triggers GET /api/search?q={query} via searchDocuments() utility.
Replaces document list with search results.
Empty query is ignored (no-op).
javaEquivalent: Angular (ngSubmit) calls Angular DocumentService.search(q) → HTTP GET to Spring @RestController @GetMapping('/api/search'); results bound to @for loop in document list component

name: Document Grid
notes: React class component (~800 lines). God component containing: API calls, filtering/sorting logic, inline styles, embedded tag editor modal, drag-and-drop upload.
Loads documents via GET /api/documents on componentDidMount.
Supports two view modes: 'grid' (card tiles) and 'list' (table rows).
Client-side filter by file type and text query (name/tags match).
Client-side sort by date (default), name, or type.
Empty state: '📂 No documents found — Upload a document to get started'.
Exports formatFileSize() utility function imported by DocumentCard and PreviewPanel (anti-pattern).
javaEquivalent: Angular 19 DocumentListComponent at /documents; data loaded via Angular DocumentService (HttpClient GET /api/documents from Spring @RestController); filtering/sorting as Angular pipes or computed signals; child components for card, list-row, upload-area, tag-editor-dialog

name: Document Grid Controls
notes: Header bar with inline filter controls and view/sort actions.
Fields: searchQuery (text input, placeholder='Filter...'), fileType (select: All Types / PDF / JPEG / PNG).
Both filter fields fire onChange immediately — no submit button for filtering.
View toggle and refresh buttons also in this bar.
All filtering is client-side against already-loaded document list.
javaEquivalent: Angular 19 form with reactive FormControl bindings; valueChanges pipe triggers client-side filter via Angular signal or pipe; no HTTP call on filter change

name: Text Filter Input
notes: onChange fires handleFilterChange({ searchQuery: value }).
Filters document list client-side: matches document.name and document.tags (case-insensitive contains).
No API call — filters already-loaded documents.
javaEquivalent: Angular FormControl with valueChanges subscription; Angular pipe or computed signal filters the documents array client-side

name: File Type Filter
notes: onChange fires handleFilterChange({ fileType: value }).
Options: '' (All Types), 'application/pdf', 'image/jpeg', 'image/png'.
Filters document list client-side by exact file_type match.
No API call — filters already-loaded documents.
javaEquivalent: Angular FormControl (select) with valueChanges subscription; Angular pipe or computed signal filters documents by fileType field

name: Grid/List View Toggle
notes: onClick fires handleViewToggle(); toggles state.viewMode between 'grid' and 'list'.
Grid mode: CSS grid with auto-fill columns. List mode: flex column rows.
Button label: '☰' in grid mode (to switch to list), '⊞' in list mode (to switch to grid).
No API call — pure UI state change.
javaEquivalent: Angular (click) toggles a viewMode signal between 'grid' and 'list'; @if/@switch in template switches between grid and list Angular sub-components

name: Refresh Documents Button
notes: onClick fires handleRefresh() → loadDocuments().
Calls GET /api/documents; replaces entire document list.
Sets loading=true during fetch.
javaEquivalent: Angular (click) calls Angular DocumentService.getDocuments() HTTP GET to Spring @RestController @GetMapping('/api/documents'); result updates documents signal/BehaviorSubject

name: Document Upload Area
notes: Drag-and-drop target zone rendered above document list.
Accepts: .pdf, .jpg, .jpeg, .png (enforced by accept attribute and backend fileFilter).
Visual states: default (dashed border) and isDragging (highlighted dashed border).
On successful upload: calls loadDocuments() to refresh list; shows '✅ Upload complete!' for 2 seconds.
On error: shows error message inline.
Sends FormData with fields: file, name to POST /api/upload.
Note: POST /api/upload writes to documents table; a DB trigger copies to documents_v2 but drops tags.
javaEquivalent: Angular 19 drag-and-drop upload component; (drop) and (click) handlers call Angular UploadService HTTP POST multipart/form-data to Spring @RestController @PostMapping('/api/upload') with @RequestParam MultipartFile file

name: Drag-and-Drop Upload
notes: onDrop fires handleDrop() → uploadFile(file).
Only first file is processed (files[0]).
Calls POST /api/upload with multipart FormData.
On success: reloads document list.
javaEquivalent: Angular (drop) event handler on CDK drop zone or native drag events; calls Angular UploadService HTTP POST multipart to Spring @RestController

name: Click to Select File
notes: onClick fires handleFileInputClick() which triggers hidden file input click.
Hidden file input accepts .pdf,.jpg,.jpeg,.png.
onChange fires handleFileInputChange() → uploadFile(file).
Calls POST /api/upload with multipart FormData.
javaEquivalent: Angular (click) on upload button triggers hidden <input type=file> click; (change) handler calls Angular UploadService HTTP POST to Spring @RestController

name: Document List
notes: Renders documents after client-side filter and sort applied.
Grid mode: card tiles via renderCard() — fields shown: file icon, name, file type label, upload date, preview link (if previewable), tags.
List mode: compact rows via renderListRow() — fields: file icon, name, type+date+tag count, tag edit button.
Empty state: '📂 No documents found — Upload a document to get started'.
Loading state: 'Loading documents...' spinner text.
Error state: error message + Retry button.
Data source: GET /api/documents (all), or GET /api/search?q={query} (search results).
javaEquivalent: Angular 19 @for loop over documents signal; card view as DocumentCardComponent @Input(), list view as DocumentListRowComponent @Input(); loaded via Angular DocumentService from Spring @RestController @GetMapping('/api/documents')

name: Select Document
notes: onClick fires handleDocumentClick(doc).
Sets state.selectedDocument; applies selected card style.
Calls onDocumentSelect(doc) prop → App.handleDocumentSelect() → shows PreviewPanel.
javaEquivalent: Angular (click) on document card emits @Output() documentSelected event; parent App component updates selectedDocument signal and shows PreviewPanelComponent

name: Edit Tags Button
notes: 🏷️ button on each document card and list row.
onClick (with e.stopPropagation()) fires handleOpenTagEditor(e, doc).
Sets tagEditorVisible=true, tagEditorDocId=doc.id, tagEditorTags=doc.tags.
Opens inline tag editor modal within DocumentGrid.
javaEquivalent: Angular (click) on tag edit button opens Angular MatDialog (or CDK Dialog) TagEditorDialogComponent; passes document id and current tags as dialog data

name: Retry on Error
notes: Shown only when error state is non-null.
onClick fires handleRefresh() → loadDocuments().
Calls GET /api/documents; clears error state on success.
javaEquivalent: Angular (click) calls Angular DocumentService.getDocuments() again; error state cleared on success response

name: Tag Editor Modal
notes: Fixed-position overlay modal rendered inside DocumentGrid (not a separate component).
Visible when state.tagEditorVisible is true.
Fields: tag text input (maxLength=50, Enter key adds tag), list of current tags with × remove buttons.
Maximum 10 tags per document enforced client-side and server-side.
Click on backdrop (overlay) closes modal without saving.
On save: calls PUT /api/documents/{id}/tags with tags array; updates local document list state.
javaEquivalent: Angular MatDialog TagEditorDialogComponent; dialog data: { documentId, tags }; (save) calls Angular TagService HTTP PUT to Spring @RestController @PutMapping('/api/documents/{id}/tags'); afterClosed() updates parent document list

name: Add Tag Button
notes: onClick fires handleAddTag(); also triggered by Enter key in tag input.
Adds trimmed non-empty, non-duplicate tag to tagEditorTags if count < 10.
No API call — modifies local modal state only.
javaEquivalent: Angular (click) pushes new tag to FormArray; Validators.max(10) enforces tag count limit

name: Save Tags Button
notes: onClick fires handleSaveTags().
Calls PUT /api/documents/{tagEditorDocId}/tags with { tags: tagEditorTags }.
On success: updates document in local documents array; closes modal.
Button disabled and shows 'Saving...' while request is in-flight.
On error: logs to console; modal stays open.
javaEquivalent: Angular (click) calls Angular TagService HTTP PUT to Spring @RestController @PutMapping('/api/documents/{id}/tags') with @RequestBody @Valid TagUpdateRequest; @Size(max=10) on tags field; closes dialog on success

name: Document Preview Panel
notes: React class component. Conditionally rendered by App when showPreview=true.
Displays selected document metadata: name, file_type, file_size (formatted).
PDF preview: uses react-pdf <Document>/<Page> with pdf.js worker from CDN. Shows page X of N.
Image preview: renders with src from GET /api/documents/{id}/preview.
Other file types: 'Preview not available for this file type.'
Empty state (no document selected): 'Select a document to preview'.
Preview URL: GET /api/documents/{id}/preview — streams file binary with Content-Type header.
javaEquivalent: Angular 19 PreviewPanelComponent receiving @Input() selectedDocument; PDF rendered via ng2-pdf-viewer or ngx-extended-pdf-viewer; image via ; preview URL calls Spring @RestController @GetMapping('/api/documents/{id}/preview') which streams file with ResponseEntity

name: Close Preview Button
notes: onClick fires onClose() prop → App.handleClosePreview().
Clears selectedDocument; sets showPreview=false; hides PreviewPanel.
No API call.
javaEquivalent: Angular (click) emits @Output() closed event; parent App component sets showPreview = false, hiding PreviewPanelComponent via @if

name: Standalone Upload Button
notes: Standalone React functional component. Not mounted in App.jsx — exists as an alternative upload implementation alongside DocumentGrid's built-in upload area.
Hidden file input accepts .pdf,.jpg,.jpeg,.png.
On file select: sends FormData (file, name) to POST /api/upload via uploadDocument().
Status messages: 'Uploading...' during upload, 'Uploaded: {name}' on success, 'Upload failed: {error}' on error.
Calls onUploadComplete(response.data) prop on success.
javaEquivalent: Angular 19 UploadButtonComponent; (change) on hidden file input calls Angular UploadService HTTP POST multipart to Spring @RestController @PostMapping('/api/upload'); emits @Output() uploadComplete on success

name: Upload Document Button
notes: onClick fires handleClick() which triggers hidden file input click.
Button disabled and shows 'Uploading...' during upload.
Accepted file types: .pdf, .jpg, .jpeg, .png.
javaEquivalent: Angular (click) programmatically clicks hidden <input type=file>; onChange calls Angular UploadService HTTP POST to Spring @RestController

name: Standalone Tag Editor
notes: Standalone React functional component. Not mounted in App.jsx — exists as an alternative tag editor alongside DocumentGrid's built-in inline tag editor modal.
Props: documentId, initialTags (array), onTagsUpdated callback.
Fields: tag text input (maxLength=50, Enter key adds tag), list of current tags with × remove buttons.
Maximum 10 tags enforced client-side (tags.length < 10 check).
Calls PUT /api/documents/{documentId}/tags via updateTags() on save.
javaEquivalent: Angular 19 TagEditorComponent receiving @Input() documentId and @Input() initialTags; emits @Output() tagsUpdated; calls Angular TagService HTTP PUT to Spring @RestController @PutMapping('/api/documents/{id}/tags')

name: Add Tag Button
notes: onClick fires handleAdd(); also triggered by Enter key in tag input.
Adds trimmed non-empty, non-duplicate tag if count < 10.
No API call — updates local component state only.
javaEquivalent: Angular (click) pushes new tag to FormArray; client-side validation enforces max 10 tags

name: Save Tags Button
notes: onClick fires handleSave().
Calls updateTags(documentId, tags) → PUT /api/documents/{documentId}/tags with { tags }.
On success: calls onTagsUpdated(tags) prop.
Button disabled and shows 'Saving...' while request is in-flight.
On error: logs to console.
javaEquivalent: Angular (click) calls Angular TagService HTTP PUT to Spring @RestController @PutMapping('/api/documents/{id}/tags') with @RequestBody @Valid TagUpdateRequest; emits @Output() tagsUpdated on success

name: Document Card
notes: Standalone React functional component. Not used by DocumentGrid (which renders cards inline via renderCard()); exists as a separate alternative card implementation.
Props: document (object with name, file_type, tags, uploaded_at), onClick callback.
Displays: document name, file type, upload date (via formatDate), tags as chips, 'No tags' italic text when no tags.
Imports formatFileSize from DocumentGrid.jsx (anti-pattern: utility exported from god component).
onClick fires onClick(document) prop.
javaEquivalent: Angular 19 DocumentCardComponent receiving @Input() document: DocumentDto; emits @Output() selected EventEmitter on click; tag chips rendered with @for loop
